subject: Mathematics
title: Infinity Is Not a Number
description: A 60-second lesson on Infinity Is Not a Number.

hook.text: Infinity plus one is still infinity. Infinity minus infinity is undefined. What kind of thing is this?

buildup.text: Infinity doesn't follow the rules of arithmetic. You can't add, subtract, or divide it normally.

discovery.text: In math, infinity is a concept about unbounded growth, not a value on the number line.

twist.text: Dividing by zero doesn't give infinity—it's undefined. But limits can 'approach' infinity.

climax.text: Calculus made infinity rigorous by using limits instead of treating it as a number.

punchline.text: Infinity is an idea, not a number you can compute with.

quiz.question: Why is infinity not considered a number?
options: It doesn't follow normal arithmetic rules
It's too large to write down
Nobody has counted to it yet
